steps.title: Multiply both sides by 12/b to clear the fraction
steps.content:
Start with $ak = \frac{b}{12}(8 + m)$. Multiply both sides by $\frac{12}{b}$:
$$\frac{12}{b} \cdot ak = \frac{12}{b} \cdot \frac{b}{12}(8 + m)$$
The right side simplifies to $8 + m$:
$$\frac{12ak}{b} = 8 + m$$

steps.title: Subtract 8 to isolate m
steps.content:
Subtract 8 from both sides:
$$m = \frac{12ak}{b} - 8$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
